dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 5875 IH: Converting Our Waste Sustainably Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Costa (for himself, Mr. Valadao, Ms. Pingree, and Mr. Gray) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture

form.official-title: To provide for the reform and continuation of agricultural and other programs of the Department of Agriculture through fiscal year 2029, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Converting Our Waste Sustainably Act of 2025 or the COWS Act of 2025.
It is the sense of Congress that—

paragraph.text: the Alternative Manure Management Program (AMMP) is a successful program in California that supports the livestock industry and is an example of what can be done nationally in the United States through a variety of practices on responsible manure management;
the 195 existing projects under AMMP are contributing towards the reduction of 1,600,000 metric tons of greenhouse gases; and

these practices enhance sustainability and nurture continued environmental stewardship, ultimately leading to enhanced local air quality and recycled manure for use as a natural fertilizer or compost for healthier soil.

subsection.text: Section 1201(a) of the Food Security Act of 1985 (16 U.S.C. 3801(a)) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (3) through (27) as paragraphs (4) through (28), respectively; and
by inserting after paragraph (2) the following:
The term composting practice means an activity (including an activity that does not require the use of a composting facility) to produce compost from organic waste that is—

subparagraph.text: generated on a farm; or
brought to a farm from the nearby community; and

the use and active management of compost on a farm to improve water retention and soil health, subject to the condition that such use shall be in compliance with applicable Federal, State, and local laws.

subsection.text: Section 1240A of the Food Security Act of 1985 (16 U.S.C. 3839aa–1) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (1) through (10) as paragraphs (2) through (11), respectively; and
by inserting before paragraph (2), as so redesignated, the following:

The term alternative manure management practices means management measures that reduce baseline methane and nitrous oxide emissions and, where applicable, reduce nitrate leaching into groundwater or improve carbon sequestration, including—

subparagraph.text: converting to or improving pasture-based management, including management-intensive rotational grazing (as defined in section 1240L(d)(1));
compost-bedded pack barns that compost manure, slatted floor pit storage that is cleaned out at least once a month, or other similar practices as determined by the Secretary; and
solid separation, scrape and vacuum technologies, or other measures, including manure management system retrofits, that primarily avoid wet handling and storage infrastructure in conjunction with—

clause.text: other similar methods as determined by the Secretary.

subsection.text: Subparagraph (A) of section 1240A(7) of the Food Security Act of 1985 (16 U.S.C. 3839aa–1(7)), as so redesignated by subsection (a), is amended—

paragraph.text: in clause (ii), by inserting and composting practices after practices;
in clause (vi), by striking the and at the end;
by redesignating clause (vii) as clause (ix); and
by inserting after clause (vi) the following:

subsection.text: Section 1240I(2)(B)(i) of the Food Security Act of 1985 (16 U.S.C. 3839aa–21(2)(B)(i)) is amended by inserting and composting practices after drainage management systems.
Section 1240B(d)(7) of the Food Security Act of 1985 (16 U.S.C. 3839aa–2(d)(7)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in the paragraph heading, by inserting state-determined before high-priority; and
in subparagraph (A)—

subparagraph.text: in clause (iii), by striking or at the end;
in clause (iv), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; or; and
by adding at the end the following:

clause.text: increases carbon sequestration or reduces greenhouse gas emissions, including emissions of methane and nitrous oxide.

subsection.text: Section 1240B(f)(1) of the Food Security Act of 1985 (16 U.S.C. 3839aa–2(f)(1)) is amended by inserting and alternative manure management after including grazing management.
Section 1240B of the Food Security Act of 1985 (16 U.S.C. 3839aa–2) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating subsections (h) through (j) as subsection (i) through (k), respectively; and
by inserting after subsection (g) the following:

The Secretary may provide a payment for an alternative manure management practice for an amount that is up to 100 percent of the costs associated with planning, design, materials, equipment, installation, labor, management, maintenance, and training related to implementing a covered management measure.
The Secretary shall provide at least 50 percent of the amount of total payments for an alternative manure management practice in advance for all costs related to—

subparagraph.text: purchasing or contracting materials and equipment; or
any technical assistance provided by the Secretary.

paragraph.text: A contract awarded under this section shall have a term that does not exceed 3 years.

section.text: Section 1240C(b) of the Food Security Act of 1985 (16 U.S.C. 3839aa–3(b)) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (1) through (4) as subparagraphs (A) through (D), respectively, and by indenting such subparagraphs 2 ems to the right;

in the matter preceding subparagraph (A), as redesignated by paragraph (1), by striking In evaluating applications under this subchapter, the Secretary shall prioritize applications— and inserting the following:
In evaluating applications under this subchapter, the Secretary shall prioritize applications—
by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: The Secretary shall develop criteria for evaluating and ranking alternative manure management contract offers to prioritize applications that will maximize—

clause.text: the overall environmental and public health benefits, including improved water quality and quantity.

subparagraph.text: In awarding alternative manure management applications, the Secretary shall develop criteria to ensure—

clause.text: scale diversity, including support for small and medium sized dairy and livestock operations; and
participation by operations with a demonstrated track record of adopting conservation measures and reducing greenhouse gas emissions.

subparagraph.text: The Secretary shall establish procedures under which—

clause.text: a group of producers may submit a joint application for a shared composting facility; and
the Secretary allocates payments to each eligible producer associated with that joint contract.

subparagraph.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall award the majority of alternative manure contracts finalized each fiscal year to offers from small and mid-sized dairy and livestock operations, including—

clause.text: limited resource farmers and ranchers; and

section.text: Section 1240F of the Food Security Act of 1985 (16 U.S.C. 3839aa–6) is amended by—

paragraph.text: inserting (a) In general.— before To the extent appropriate, the Secretary shall assist a producer in achieving the conservation and environmental goals of a program plan by—; and
inserting at the end the following:

subsection.text: For alternative manure management contracts, the Secretary shall—

paragraph.text: determine and publish factors for estimating the carbon sequestrations and greenhouse gas emissions reductions for each alternative manure management practice;

identify eligible producers based on manure management practices used at the time of application, including the anaerobic decomposition of volatile solids stored in a lagoon or other predominantly liquid anaerobic environment and their ability to replace or complement that anaerobic system with an alternative manure management practice; and
assist an eligible producer in achieving the carbon sequestration, greenhouse gas emissions reduction, and other environmental and public health goals of the alternative manure management systems by—

subparagraph.text: providing payments for designing and implementing one or more alternative manure management practices;
providing that eligible producer with information, technical assistance, and training to aid in implementation of the alternative manure management practices;
reviewing the adequacy of existing conservation practice standards for supporting the alternative manure management practices and, if necessary—

clause.text: revising existing conservation practice standards; and
developing new conservation practice standards; and

subparagraph.text: entering into cooperative agreements with third-party technical assistance providers with relevant expertise in alternative manure management systems to ensure adequate technical services are available to alternative manure management applicants.

section.text: Section 1240G of the Food Security Act of 1985 (16 U.S.C. 3839aa–7) is amended—

paragraph.text: by inserting (a) In general.— before Not including payments; and
by striking , regardless of the number of contracts entered into under this subchapter by the person or legal entity and inserting at the end the following:

waive the applicability of the limitations in section 1001D(b) or section 1240(G) for a payment made under an alternative manure management contract if the Secretary determines that the waiver is necessary to fulfill the objectives of the project; and
impose a separate payment limitation for the contract with respect to which the waiver applies.

section.text: Section 1240H(c)(1)(B)(i) of the Food Security Act of 1985 (16 U.S.C. 3839aa–8(c)(1)(B)(i)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subclause (VI), by striking and at the end; and
by inserting at the end the following:

section.text: Section 1242(h) of the Food Security Act of 1985 (16 U.S.C. 3842(h)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: Not later than one year after the date of enactment of this paragraph, the Secretary shall—

subparagraph.text: conduct a review of the composting facilities practice standard and the soil carbon amendment practice standard to determine their adequacy in advancing alternative manure management and on-farm composting generation and utilization; and
develop and implement a new conservation practice standard for the on-farm production of compost.
